mission.title: Install and Configure Kubevirt on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeVirt is a virtual machine management add-on for Kubernetes that allows you to define and manage virtual machines using Kubernetes' Custom Resource Definitions. Installing KubeVirt enables you to run virtual machines alongside your container workloads in a Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Install KubeVirt operator
steps.description:
Deploy the KubeVirt operator from the official release:
```bash
export KUBEVIRT_VERSION=v1.5.0
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}/kubevirt-operator.yaml
```

steps.title: Deploy KubeVirt custom resource
steps.description:
Create the KubeVirt CR to deploy the control plane:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}/kubevirt-cr.yaml
```
Wait for all components to be ready:
```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=600s kubevirt/kubevirt -n kubevirt
```

steps.title: Install virtctl CLI
steps.description:
Install the virtctl CLI for managing virtual machines:
```bash
curl -Lo virtctl https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}/virtctl-${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}-linux-amd64
chmod +x virtctl
sudo mv virtctl /usr/local/bin/
```

steps.title: Create a test VM
steps.description:
Deploy a test virtual machine:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://kubevirt.io/labs/manifests/vm.yaml
virtctl start testvm
virtctl console testvm
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt
kubectl get kubevirt -n kubevirt
kubectl get vms
virtctl version
```

uninstall.title: Delete VMs
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl delete vms --all
kubectl delete vmis --all
```

upgrade.title: Apply new operator version
upgrade.description:
```bash
export NEW_VERSION=v1.5.0
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${NEW_VERSION}/kubevirt-operator.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${NEW_VERSION}/kubevirt-cr.yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Virt-handler pods not ready
troubleshooting.description:
KubeVirt requires hardware virtualization:
```bash
kubectl logs -l kubevirt.io=virt-handler -n kubevirt
virt-host-validate qemu
```

troubleshooting.title: VM fails to start
troubleshooting.description:
Check the VMI status:
```bash
kubectl describe vmi testvm
```
Common issue: nodes don't support nested virtualization.

troubleshooting.title: Performance issues
troubleshooting.description:
Enable hardware acceleration:
```bash
kubectl get kubevirt -n kubevirt -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.configuration.developerConfiguration.useEmulation}'
```
Set `useEmulation: false` for production.

resolution.summary: KubeVirt is installed with the operator, control plane, and virtctl CLI for running virtual machines on Kubernetes.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
